dimensionDescriptions.lifecycle_initial_access: Explain the most defensible entry point and the evidence that supports that sequencing.
dimensionDescriptions.lifecycle_execution: Explain where malicious execution likely occurred and why that execution assessment matters.
dimensionDescriptions.lifecycle_persistence: Explain the foothold that most likely sustained attacker access over time.
dimensionDescriptions.lifecycle_lateral_movement: Explain how access likely moved between systems, identities, or environments.
dimensionDescriptions.lifecycle_objective_impact: Explain the most likely attacker objective and potential operational impact if the chain continued.
dimensionDescriptions.detection_gap: Explain the most critical visibility gap and how it constrained analytic certainty.
dimensionDescriptions.mitigation_breakpoint: Explain which interruption point would most effectively break the intrusion chain.
dimensionDescriptions.evidence_linkage: Explain which evidence items directly support each major analytic claim.
dimensionDescriptions.uncertainty_collection: Explain what data to collect next to reduce uncertainty in the current narrative.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Explain why the selected risk level follows from likely impact, exposure, and attacker opportunity.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Explain why the selected confidence level is justified by corroboration and remaining gaps.
dimensionDescriptions.tradeoff: Explain operational tradeoffs of the selected defensive actions and feasibility constraints.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary (operationally framed)
reportSections.template: In 3–5 sentences, state the leading intrusion narrative, current operational risk, confidence level, and the highest-priority decision for leadership now.

reportSections.title: Intrusion Narrative (staged timeline across lifecycle)
reportSections.template: Describe a coherent timeline from initial access through execution, persistence, lateral movement, and likely objective/impact, noting where sequencing is uncertain.

reportSections.title: Evidence Summary (3–6 key items, why each matters)
reportSections.template: List 3–6 strongest evidence items and briefly explain how each item supports, challenges, or qualifies your lifecycle narrative.

reportSections.title: Primary Hypothesis and Alternatives (why yours is most defensible)
reportSections.template: State your selected primary hypothesis, then compare at least one plausible alternative and justify why your choice is currently most defensible.

reportSections.title: Detection Gap Analysis (most critical gap, how it affected confidence)
reportSections.template: Identify the single most critical visibility gap, explain how it constrained confidence, and specify the practical consequence for response decisions.

reportSections.title: Mitigation Breakpoints (top 1–2 breakpoints, tradeoffs and feasibility)
reportSections.template: Recommend the top 1–2 breakpoints to disrupt the chain, then state implementation tradeoffs, dependencies, and near-term feasibility.

reportSections.title: Collection Plan (what to collect next to reduce uncertainty)
reportSections.template: Name 1–2 immediate collection actions and explain how each likely result would increase, decrease, or preserve your current confidence level.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion (next 24–72 hour actions)
reportSections.template: Close with concrete 24–72 hour actions, responsible owners, and trigger conditions that would require reassessment.

gapRules.prompt: Consider identifying the most critical detection gap, explaining the consequence of that blind spot, and noting one practical remedy that could reduce the gap.
You may want to describe at least one mitigation breakpoint that could disrupt the chain and include the main operational tradeoff.
Consider including one or two immediate collection actions and explaining how each outcome could change your confidence.
You could strengthen end-to-end lifecycle coverage by presenting a coherent narrative from initial access through objective and impact, including at least one transition rationale.
It may help to justify your confidence level by connecting it to corroborated evidence and unresolved uncertainty.
